shots.title: MCP setup hints for hook activation
shots.notes: Added setup hints to MCP search/execute tools showing how to activate guards via slope init. 11 new MCP tests covering hint content and formatting.

shots.title: Subagent-gate guard
shots.notes: PreToolUse guard on Task that forces haiku model and caps max_turns on Explore/Plan subagents. Exempts resumed agents. 8 tests covering model validation, turns limits, custom config, and passthrough cases.

shots.title: Push-nudge guard
shots.notes: PostToolUse guard on Bash that fires after git commit commands. Nudges based on unpushed count, time since push, and main branch detection. 4 tests.

shots.title: Workflow-gate guard + compaction upgrade
shots.notes: Workflow-gate: PreToolUse on ExitPlanMode, blocks until review rounds complete per review-state.json. Compaction upgrade: replaced single-event marker with full handoff (git state, sprint context, claims) to .slope/handoffs/. 10 tests across both guards.

nutrition.description: Clean build + typecheck + all 1046 tests pass (780 core + 36 store + 165 CLI + 65 MCP) after each commit
7 commits in strict dependency order: core types first, then each guard file individually, then registration + tests. Commit-per-file discipline.
All existing tests pass unchanged. 32 new tests added. Zero regressions. Only core guard count test updated (6→9).

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Efficient port from CaddyStack patterns into SLOPE's guard framework. The existing guard infrastructure (dispatcher, formatters, test patterns) made each new guard a straightforward plug-in. No architectural surprises.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: Guards need `slope init --claude-code` to activate (generates settings.json hooks). The subagent-gate defaults to haiku-only — configure guidance.subagentAllowModels in .slope/config.json to allow other models. The workflow-gate reads .slope/review-state.json which must be written by the review process (not yet implemented). Compaction handoffs go to .slope/handoffs/ (gitignored).
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: The compaction guard's dual-output design (file primary, store secondary) was the most interesting architectural choice. The 10-second guard timeout from Claude Code means store writes can fail — file writes are the reliable fallback.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: The guard framework now has 9 guards covering the full agent lifecycle: exploration, editing, committing, pushing, subagent spawning, plan review, and session end. Next logical step is wiring the review-state.json writer into the review command.

yardage_book_updates: packages/core/src/guard.ts: GuardName union extended to 9, GuidanceConfig gains 5 new fields, 3 new GUARD_DEFINITIONS

packages/cli/src/guards/push-nudge.ts: New — PostToolUse context guard for Bash
packages/cli/src/guards/workflow-gate.ts: New — PreToolUse deny guard for ExitPlanMode
packages/cli/src/guards/compaction.ts: Upgraded — full handoff with git state, sprint context, claims
packages/cli/tests/guards.test.ts: 21 new tests across 4 describe blocks
packages/mcp-tools/src/index.ts: Setup hints for guard activation in search/execute tools
packages/mcp-tools/src/index.test.ts: 11 new tests for MCP setup hints
course_management_notes: 4 shots, all in_the_hole — zero hazards, zero penalties, par
32 new tests. Total test count: 1046 (up from 1014)
